shulker_farm_build.title: Build an automated Shulker Shell farm — enclosed End City structure with spawn control and item collection

subtitle.text: Shulker Shells are the most valuable item in the End. Two shells. One chest. One Shulker Box. Build a factory.

description: CH.14 · THE SHULKER FARM
▸ Shulker Shells craft Shulker Boxes.
Shulker Boxes are the most powerful inventory item in the game.
27 stacks that function as a single inventory slot.
The target is 27 Shulker Boxes — 729 additional inventory slots.
To reach 27 boxes you need 54 shells.
To reach 54 shells you need a farm.
◈ SHULKER FARMING — BASE APPROACH
└ Natural spawn: Shulkers only in End Cities (fixed locations)
Limited supply — kill all Shulkers in a city, they do not respawn
└ Farm approach: transport Shulkers to your base
Shulker in a boat: pick up with boat, transport via Elytra
Requires: patience + boat + clear flight path over void
◈ SHULKER FARM DESIGN
└ Spawn containment: glass enclosure (Shulkers teleport but
cannot teleport to non-solid blocks)
└ Spawn condition: Shulkers do not naturally respawn
— your farm is a kill chamber for transported Shulkers
└ Transport pipeline: End City → boat capture → base chamber
└ Item collection: Hopper + Chest below kill platform
└ Projectile management: glass walls deflect Shulker bullets
(Shulker bullets bounce off glass — kill safely through gaps)
◈ SHELL ECONOMY MATH
└ Shell drop rate: 0-2 per Shulker (avg 1)
(Fortune III pickaxe: does NOT affect Shulker shell drops)
└ 2 Shells + 1 Chest = 1 Shulker Box
└ Target: 54 shells = 27 Shulker Boxes
└ With farm: 10-20 Shulkers per city × multiple cities
= realistic 54-shell collection in 3-5 city clears
▸ The Shulker Box is what makes the End worth the trip.
Not the dragon. Not the cities.
The ability to carry 729 stacks as 27 items.
Every civilization that reached the End
built its logistics on Shulker Boxes.
The farm is just recognizing that the shells are the point.
▶ Build a Shulker containment farm [check]
▶ Collect 16 Shulker Shells
◇ Shulker Box color: dyeable with any dye (right-click with dye).
Color-code your boxes by category:
purple = combat, green = farming, red = potions, yellow = building.
A well-organized Shulker Box system is one of the highest
quality-of-life upgrades in the entire pack.
